title: sovereign debt / investor composition / emerging markets | Research Paper | Who holds sovereign debt and why it matters
summary: This BIS Working Paper (No. 1099) constructs a comprehensive dataset of sovereign debt holdings across 95 countries over 20 years, categorizing investors as foreign/domestic banks, non-bank private investors, or official investors. The paper finds that private non-bank investors (mainly investment funds) absorb disproportionately more sovereign debt supply than other investor types, funding nearly 70% of increases in sovereign debt, and are the most yield-responsive investor class.
